title: Reentrancy Protection Analysis
impact: Potential reentrancy if external calls made during state updates

title: Quorum Calculation Division
impact: Potential division by zero if seats = 0

evidence.note: return 1 + (seats * 51) / 100; - division by 100 is safe but seats could be 0
_setSeats(0, seats) called in initialize with seats parameter

exploit_sketch: If seats = 0, quorum calculation returns 1 + (0 * 51) / 100 = 1
This could allow single-director execution if seats somehow become 0

title: NFT OwnerOf External Call Error Handling
impact: Potential DoS if NFT contract behaves unexpectedly
exploit_sketch: Malicious NFT contract that reverts in ownerOf()
Could prevent delegation or director status checks
If NFT contract changes behavior, could break governance
recommended_fix: Consider additional validation or fallback mechanisms

title: EIP-1271 Signature Validation
impact: Smart contract directors could bypass ownership checks

evidence.note: EIP-1271 validation allows smart contracts to act as directors

exploit_sketch: Smart contract that implements isValidSignature incorrectly
Could allow unauthorized access to director functions
EIP-1271 implementation bugs in NFT contract itself
recommended_fix: Add timeout or additional validation for EIP-1271 signatures

title: Delegation Balance Constraint
impact: Users could delegate more than their balance

evidence.note: Checks total delegation doesn't exceed balance after new delegation
_update() enforces delegation constraints on all transfers

exploit_sketch: User delegates amount, then transfers tokens away
Could leave negative delegation state
ERC4626 operations might bypass delegation checks
recommended_fix: Ensure all balance-changing operations respect delegation constraints

title: Low-Level Call in Transaction Execution
impact: Arbitrary external calls with ETH transfer

evidence.note: target.call{value: value}(data) with success check and state revert on failure
Allows address(this) calls only for upgradeImplementation

exploit_sketch: Malicious transaction calling back into Chamber
Reentrancy through external contract callbacks
ETH drain if target contract has fallback issues
recommended_fix: Already protected by nonReentrant and CEI pattern

title: Proxy Upgrade Authorization
exploit_sketch: If ProxyAdmin ownership not properly transferred
External actor could call ProxyAdmin.upgrade() directly
Bypasses governance multisig approval
recommended_fix: Ensure Registry properly transfers ProxyAdmin ownership

title: Linked List Operations Gas Limits

evidence.note: MAX_NODES = 100 limit on linked list size

exploit_sketch: Attacker creates many small delegations to fill MAX_NODES
Repositioning operations become expensive
Could prevent new delegations or cause out-of-gas
recommended_fix: Consider gas limits for operations or optimize data structure
